title: The Old Ticket
summary: Two friends argue about an old train ticket: keep it or throw it away?

body.speaker: Mei
body.text: Liam, what is this old paper in your wallet? It is a train ticket. It is from two years ago!

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: Yes, I know. I keep it. It is the ticket from my first trip to Kyoto.

body.speaker: Mei
body.text: But it is just paper. It is small and grey. You cannot use it now. Why keep it? Throw it away.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: I cannot throw it away. When I look at it, I remember the day. I remember the rain. I remember the warm tea on the train.

body.speaker: Mei
body.text: I understand that. Memories are good. But you do not need the paper. The memory is in your head, not in the ticket.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: That is true. But the ticket helps me. I am busy. I forget many things. The small ticket says, 'Remember that day.'

body.speaker: Mei
body.text: Hmm. I throw away my old tickets. But I keep photos. Maybe my photos are like your ticket. They help me remember too.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: Yes! A photo and a ticket are the same. They are small things. But they hold a big day inside.

body.speaker: Mei
body.text: But a ticket does not show the day. It only shows a place and a time. It does not show the rain or the tea.

body.speaker: Liam
body.text: You are right. The ticket is empty. But my head is not empty. The ticket is the key, and my memory is the room. I open the room with the key.

body.speaker: Mei
body.text: That is a nice idea. Okay, keep your old ticket. But please, clean your wallet sometimes!

annotations.meaningVi: một ngày trọng đại bên trong
dọn dẹp cái ví của bạn

grammarNotes.title: Why and because
grammarNotes.bodyVi: "Why" hỏi lý do; người kia có thể trả lời bằng lý do.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Mei asks "Why keep it?" and Liam gives reasons like "When I look at it, I remember the day."

grammarNotes.title: but to show contrast
grammarNotes.bodyVi: "but" nối hai ý trái ngược nhau.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: See "The ticket is empty. But my head is not empty."

grammarNotes.title: Comparison with like / the same
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng "like" và "the same" để so sánh hai thứ.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Mei says "my photos are like your ticket," and Liam says "A photo and a ticket are the same."

translationVi:
Mei: Liam, tờ giấy cũ trong ví của cậu là gì vậy? Đó là một tấm vé tàu. Nó từ hai năm trước!
Liam: Ừ, tớ biết. Tớ giữ nó. Đó là tấm vé từ chuyến đi Kyoto đầu tiên của tớ.
Mei: Nhưng nó chỉ là tờ giấy. Nó nhỏ và xám xịt. Cậu đâu dùng được nữa. Giữ làm gì? Vứt đi.
Liam: Tớ không thể vứt nó đi. Khi nhìn vào nó, tớ nhớ lại ngày hôm đó. Tớ nhớ cơn mưa. Tớ nhớ tách trà ấm trên tàu.
Mei: Tớ hiểu điều đó. Kỷ niệm là điều tốt. Nhưng cậu đâu cần tờ giấy. Ký ức nằm trong đầu cậu, không phải trong tấm vé.
Liam: Đúng vậy. Nhưng tấm vé giúp tớ. Tớ bận lắm. Tớ quên nhiều thứ. Tấm vé nhỏ này nhắc: "Hãy nhớ ngày hôm đó."
Mei: Hừm. Tớ thì vứt vé cũ. Nhưng tớ giữ ảnh. Có lẽ những tấm ảnh của tớ giống tấm vé của cậu. Chúng cũng giúp tớ nhớ.
Liam: Đúng rồi! Một tấm ảnh và một tấm vé là như nhau. Chúng là những vật nhỏ. Nhưng chúng chứa một ngày trọng đại bên trong.
Mei: Nhưng tấm vé đâu cho thấy ngày hôm đó. Nó chỉ cho thấy một nơi chốn và một thời điểm. Nó không cho thấy cơn mưa hay tách trà.
Liam: Cậu nói đúng. Tấm vé thì trống rỗng. Nhưng đầu tớ thì không trống rỗng. Tấm vé là chìa khóa, còn ký ức của tớ là căn phòng. Tớ mở căn phòng bằng chiếc chìa khóa.
Mei: Đó là một ý hay. Được rồi, giữ tấm vé cũ của cậu đi. Nhưng làm ơn, thỉnh thoảng dọn cái ví đi nhé!

questions.prompt: What is the old paper in Liam's wallet?

questions.explanation: "It is the ticket from my first trip to Kyoto."

questions.prompt: What does Mei tell Liam to do at first?
What does Liam remember when he looks at the ticket?
options: The rain and the warm tea
questions.explanation: "I remember the rain. I remember the warm tea on the train."

questions.prompt: Where does Mei say the memory really is?
options: In your head, not in the ticket
questions.explanation: "The memory is in your head, not in the ticket."

questions.prompt: What does Mei keep instead of tickets?
questions.hint: Look for where Mei changes her mind a little.

questions.prompt: What does Mei say a ticket does NOT show?
questions.explanation: "It does not show the rain or the tea."
questions.hint: Look at Mei's line about place and time.

questions.prompt: What does Liam compare the ticket to?
questions.explanation: "The ticket is the key, and my memory is the room."
questions.hint: Look near the end at Liam's image.

questions.prompt: What does 'empty' mean in 'the ticket is empty'?
options: It has no memory inside by itself
questions.explanation: The ticket only shows a place and time, so by itself it holds no memory.
questions.hint: Think about what the ticket shows and does not show.

questions.prompt: What do Liam and Mei agree about?
options: Small things can help us remember big days
questions.explanation: "They are small things. But they hold a big day inside."
questions.hint: Look at the part where they say the photo and ticket are the same.

questions.prompt: How does Mei feel about Liam by the end?
options: She wants the ticket for herself
She accepts his reason but wants a clean wallet
questions.explanation: "Okay, keep your old ticket. But please, clean your wallet sometimes!"

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of this talk?
options: A small object can be a key to a big memory
questions.explanation: "The ticket is the key, and my memory is the room."

blanks.explanation: His first trip was to Kyoto.
When he looks at it, he remembers the day.
He remembers the warm tea on the train.
The memory is in your head.
He is busy and forgets many things.
A photo and a ticket are the same.
The ticket is empty by itself.
The ticket is the key to the room of memory.
Mei asks him to clean his wallet.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Liam says "The ticket is the key, and my memory is the room." Do you keep a small old thing like this? What memory does it open for you?
